title: Cole Tomas Allen, Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Suspect, Was Propelled by Outrage, Authorities Say
summary: A man who has worked as a tutor and graduated from the California Institute of Technology is being held by authorities in connection with the armed attack at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

title: Even Before the Gunfire. Many Guests Bore the Scars of Political Violence
summary: Gunfire at a press gala on Saturday underscored the ever growing club of political figures whose lives have been upended by violence.

title: ‘Shots Fired!’: Inside the Pandemonium at the Washington Hilton
summary: Guests dived to the floor and took cover as Secret Service agents climbed over tables to protect some of the country’s most high-ranking officials, including President Trump.

title: Inside the Chaos at the Correspondents’ Dinner, and How the U.S. Mint Is Buying Drug Cartel Gold
summary: Plus, marathon runners shatter a world record.

title: The U.S. Started the War. The Rest of the World Is Feeling the Effects.
summary: In just eight weeks, much of the global economy has been knocked sideways. America has mostly been spared from the tumult.

title: China’s Economy Starts to Show Cracks From Iran War
summary: China’s strategic reserves of oil and natural gas have insulated it somewhat, but its manufacturing-based economy is beginning to falter.

title: Oil Rises and Stocks Waver as Peace Talks Stall
summary: Oil prices rose and stocks fell after President Trump called off a trip to Pakistan by two U.S. negotiators for peace talks with Iran.

title: Canada Says Its Gold Is Traceable and Clean. So We Traced It.
summary: We ended up in a Colombian mine controlled by a drug cartel.

title: U.S. Mint Buys Drug Cartel Gold and Sells It as ‘American’
summary: As prices for the precious metal soar, the industry’s guardrails have broken down.

title: How We Traced U.S. Government Gold to a Drug Cartel
summary: Three reporters followed supply chains to reveal that the U.S. Mint buys gold that comes from foreign pawn shops and drug dealers, then claims it is from the United States.

title: Supreme Court Reviews Police Use of Cell Location Data to Find Criminals
summary: Geofence searches allow law enforcement to find suspects and witnesses by sweeping up location data from cellphone users near crime scenes.

title: Supreme Court to Hear Arguments in Landmark Roundup Weedkiller Case
summary: A victory for the manufacturer, Bayer, could end thousands of lawsuits against the company claiming that the herbicide causes cancer.

title: The Best Los Angeles Restaurants
summary: Our current favorites in one of the greatest food cities in the world.

title: Best Miami Restaurants
summary: The culinary capital of South Florida has outstanding Cuban and Caribbean cuisine — of course — but also world-class Japanese, Italian, Ethiopian and more.

title: Best New Orleans Restaurants
summary: There’s plenty of great Creole and Cajun food, of course, but also outstanding Vietnamese, Indian and Trinbagonian cuisine.

title: Can King Charles Help Heal the U.S.-British Rupture?
summary: Not since his mother, Queen Elizabeth II, traveled to Washington after the Suez Crisis has a visit by the British monarch come at such a fraught time in Anglo-American relations.

title: 8 Memorable Moments From Past British Royal Visits to the U.S.
summary: With King Charles III and Queen Camilla set to arrive for a state visit with President Trump, we look back at some major moments in previous royal trips to the United States.

title: Musk vs. Altman: A High-Stakes A.I. Clash Goes to Court on Monday
summary: Elon Musk is seeking more than $150 billion in damages and a complete shake-up of OpenAI. The outcome could have big consequences for the artificial intelligence industry.

title: Bernie Sanders Pressures Democrats to Reject Super PAC Support in Primaries
summary: The independent senator from Vermont says top Democrats should tell candidates they will otherwise lose access to party resources.

title: What to Watch Next in the Nationwide Redistricting Battle
summary: All eyes are on Florida and Virginia, where a special session and courtroom proceedings set for this week factor into the battle for the House in the midterms.

title: Eric Swalwell, Tony Gonzales and Accountability in the Post-Post-#MeToo Era
summary: What constitutes unacceptable behavior? And how many accusers does it take for an allegation to be believed? Societal standards remain nebulous.

title: Homeowners Fight for Control of Their Community in China
summary: They signed petitions, organized rallies and held strategy sessions over karaoke, debating how far to push the authorities in their dispute with a developer.

title: The Rising Chinese Automaker Not Named BYD
summary: Geely is challenging the giant BYD by adapting quickly to swings in demand and energy prices, seizing on interest in electric vehicles prompted by the war in Iran.

title: China Will Require Meta to Unwind Acquisition of AI Start-Up Manus
summary: The impact of the ruling was not immediately clear, but it could send a chilling signal to Chinese tech founders seeking to team up with foreign companies.

title: Oprah Winfrey Moves Her Podcast (and Books and Products) to Amazon
summary: The multiyear deal with Ms. Winfrey signifies the tech giant’s growing ambitions in video podcasts.

title: California Billionaire Tax Has Enough Signatures to Land on Ballot, Backers Say
summary: The measure calls for placing a one-time 5 percent tax on the assets of California residents with at least $1.1 billion. Opponents are backing competing measures to counter the tax.

title: New Taxes Helped Cool London’s Housing Market. Could That Happen in New York?
summary: Economists and real estate agents are calling London’s taxation of wealthy property owners a cautionary tale for New York, where leaders have endorsed a second-home tax.

title: Are Movies Really Getting Darker? Let’s Shine Some Light on the Issue.
summary: Movie watchers are often left wondering why today’s films look murkier than films of years past. Here’s how some experts diagnose the issue.

title: Why Trump Wants Unqualified U.S. Attorneys
summary: Revenge-based criminal cases against the president’s enemies have not come to fruition. That’s where the U.S. attorneys come in.

title: Gold Standards
summary: We explore a Times investigation into the gold market.

title: 60 Countries to Meet on Phasing Out Fossil Fuels but Are Excluding the U.S.
summary: The Trump administration was not invited to the gathering in Santa Marta, Colombia. A White House spokeswoman called the green transition “destructive.”

title: Raghu Rai, Photographer Who Captured Modern India, Dies at 83
summary: Mr. Rai documented major figures like the Dalai Lama and Indira Gandhi, as well as the victims of a poison gas leak in the city of Bhopal.

title: Beekeeper Gets Jail After a Swarm Is Unleashed During an Eviction
summary: The woman, who is a beekeeper, was sentenced to six months after being convicted of using the bees as a weapon against deputies during a 2022 eviction.
